summary: Matched additional sysdolphin baselib aobj code by replacing hand-written assembly for TObjForeachAnim and RObjForeachAnim with C implementations. The PR also added the required robj.h and tobj.h includes and updated the TObjForeachAnim prototype in aobj.h. Evidence is limited to the diff; there were no PR body notes or review comments in the provided slice.
what_changed: Added includes for sysdolphin/baselib/robj.h and sysdolphin/baselib/tobj.h in aobj.c so the C implementations can access HSD_RObj and HSD_TObj fields.
Replaced asm void TObjForeachAnim(void) with a typed C function: void TObjForeachAnim(HSD_TObj* tobj, s32 flags, void* r5, s32 r6, void* r7).
Implemented TObjForeachAnim as a linked-list walk over HSD_TObj nodes using tobj->next, checking flags & 0x400 and tobj->aobj before calling callbackForeachFunc.
Replaced asm void RObjForeachAnim(void) with a typed C function: void RObjForeachAnim(HSD_RObj* robj, s32 flags, void* r5, s32 r6, void* r7).
Implemented RObjForeachAnim as a linked-list walk over HSD_RObj nodes using robj->next, checking flags & 0x200 and robj->aobj before calling callbackForeachFunc.
Updated the aobj.h declaration for TObjForeachAnim from an untyped void prototype to one using struct _HSD_TObj*, s32 flags, and the remaining callback argument parameters.
smart_moves: The C loops mirror the original assembly control flow closely: early loop condition, flag mask check, aobj null check, callback call, then advance to next.
The implementation preserved the numeric HSD_Type values passed to callbackForeachFunc: 0xB for TObj and 0xA for RObj.
Including robj.h and tobj.h allowed field-based C code instead of relying on raw offsets from the assembly, making the matched functions more maintainable.
The author kept unknown or weakly understood callback parameters as generic void*/s32 names derived from registers, avoiding overclaiming semantics not proven by the diff.
decomp_lessons: Small list-walking assembly functions are good candidates for direct C replacement when struct fields line up with observed offsets.
When matching foreach helpers, preserve flag masks and type discriminator constants exactly; here TObj used 0x400 and type 0xB, while RObj used 0x200 and type 0xA.
Adding the correct subsystem headers can unlock struct field access and reduce handwritten assembly without requiring broad refactors.
If callback argument semantics are not yet clear, typed placeholders such as void* and s32 can still be useful for matching while avoiding speculative names.
Header prototypes should be improved alongside C matches so future callers and decomp work have more accurate function signatures.
naming_conventions: Existing baselib naming style uses Pascal-like subsystem prefixes such as TObjForeachAnim, RObjForeachAnim, HSD_AObjAlloc, and HSD_AObjSetRate.
Struct pointer typedefs follow HSD_* naming where available, such as HSD_TObj* and HSD_RObj* in C definitions.
The header declaration used struct _HSD_TObj* rather than HSD_TObj*, likely because of available forward declarations or include constraints in aobj.h.
Unknown callback parameters were left unnamed in the header as void*, s32, void*, while the C definition used register-derived names r5, r6, and r7.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted two nofralloc assembly functions into structured while loops over linked lists.
Mapped TObj assembly offset 0x64 to tobj->aobj and offset 0x08 to tobj->next.
Mapped RObj assembly offset 0x18 to robj->aobj and offset 0x00 to robj->next.
Matched rlwinm-derived flag tests as flags & 0x400 for TObjForeachAnim and flags & 0x200 for RObjForeachAnim.
Matched callbackForeachFunc call argument order as aobj, object pointer, type constant, function pointer/argument payload, AObj_Arg_Type-like value, and callbackArg-like pointer.
Preserved one cast in TObjForeachAnim, passing (s32) r6 to callbackForeachFunc, suggesting a signature or codegen-sensitive type detail.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that further type callbackForeachFunc parameters or replace the remaining func_803647DC and func_80364924 assembly in aobj.c.
Check subsequent changes to aobj.h for whether RObjForeachAnim receives a public prototype or whether TObjForeachAnim signature is refined.
Search for uses of flag masks 0x400 and 0x200 in baselib animation traversal to confirm naming or enum opportunities.
Compare later HSD_TObj and HSD_RObj struct definitions to validate the field offsets used here: TObj aobj at 0x64, TObj next at 0x08, RObj aobj at 0x18, RObj next at 0x00.
